traps.description: refill() updates bucket.tokens with `tokens + added` but never caps at `capacity`. After a long idle period the bucket can hold far more than `capacity` tokens, letting a single caller burst past the intended rate limit.
traps.summary: refill() doesn't cap tokens at capacity, allowing bursts past the rate limit.

traps.description: Constructor does not validate `opts.capacity > 0`, `opts.refillPerSec >= 0`, or that either value is finite. Zero/negative/NaN capacity silently produces a limiter whose math is nonsense (always denies or yields NaN), instead of throwing a clear error at construction time.
traps.summary: Constructor doesn't validate capacity/refill, so bad config silently breaks the limiter.

traps.description: The async slow path of acquire() polls `msUntilTokens` and sleeps. When `refillPerSec === 0` the bucket can never refill, but msUntilTokens returns a constant 1000 ms, so the loop sleeps forever instead of rejecting. A caller awaiting acquire() in this state hangs indefinitely.
traps.summary: With refillPerSec=0 the async acquire() sleeps forever instead of rejecting.
